title: UC3 — Lunar rover telemetry won't keep its uplink up
description: Today's mission for the Artemis pad shift: bring the lunar rover online, run a telemetry stream up to base, then hand the diagnosis to artemis-rover-telemetry-debugger through the kagent dashboard chat.

steps.title: Mission setup — bring the rover online and stream telemetry
steps.explanation:
The lunar rover is checking in for the Artemis pad shift. Bring it online on your vCluster and run a short stream of telemetry bursts to it — the observability bundle that watches the rover's vitals (Prometheus + Grafana, plus a kagent ↔ observability namespace bridge), then the rover's namespace + service + deployment, the on-call debugger agent, and finally a transient operator station that pushes 30 bursts to the rover's outbound queue.

Fourteen files are dropped into your workspace by this step (`fileEdits`): ten under `infra/observability/`, three under `uc3/manifests/`, and one under `uc3/agents/`. The commands below send them to the cluster in order — observability first, then UC3 on top of it. Once the rover Pod is `Ready`, a one-shot `telemetry-stream` Pod runs the burst loop against the rover's in-cluster Service DNS and self-deletes when the loop completes.

The operator station's output is the per-burst response from the rover (`{"size_mb":N}` — its outbound buffer count). The next step will tell you whether the rover is keeping its uplink up across the stream.

fileEdits.content:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: artemis-observability
  labels:
    kagent-workshop/component: observability

# infra/observability/ — vanilla Prometheus + Grafana single-replica install
# shared by UC3 (FR-012) and UC4. No operator (NFR-006); emptyDir storage
# (the workshop is ephemeral); anonymous-admin Grafana (per-participant
# vCluster contains the exposure). See STORY-018 + docs/architecture-…md §C5.
apiVersion: kustomize.config.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Kustomization

namespace: artemis-observability

resources:
  - 00-namespace.yaml
  - prometheus/10-rbac.yaml
  - prometheus/20-configmap-scrape.yaml
  - prometheus/30-deployment.yaml
  - prometheus/40-service.yaml
  - grafana/20-configmap-datasource.yaml
  - grafana/30-deployment.yaml
  - grafana/40-service.yaml

labels:
  - includeSelectors: false
    pairs:
      kagent-workshop/component: observability

# RBAC for Prometheus' kubernetes_sd_configs (role: endpoints).
#
# Prom needs cluster-scoped read on services / endpoints / pods / nodes to
# resolve the targets the scrape config selects via the `monitoring=prom`
# Service label. This is the minimum verb set documented for the `endpoints`
# SD role; trimming further breaks discovery silently.
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: prometheus-server
  namespace: artemis-observability
  labels:
    app: prometheus-server
    kagent-workshop/component: observability
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: artemis-observability-prometheus
  labels:
    kagent-workshop/component: observability
rules:
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources: ["services", "endpoints", "pods", "nodes", "nodes/metrics"]
    verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
  - nonResourceURLs: ["/metrics"]
    verbs: ["get"]
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRoleBinding
metadata:
  name: artemis-observability-prometheus
  labels:
    kagent-workshop/component: observability
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: artemis-observability-prometheus
subjects:
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: prometheus-server
    namespace: artemis-observability

# Prometheus scrape config.
#
# Two jobs:
#   1. `prometheus`         — self-scrape on localhost:9090 so the Prom UI shows
#                             at least one healthy target before any UC ships.
#   2. `kubernetes-services` — `kubernetes_sd_configs: role: endpoints` filtered
#                             to Services labelled `monitoring=prom`. This is
#                             how UC3 / UC4 opt into being scraped without any
#                             per-UC scrape-config edits (architecture §C5).
#
# Workshop-fast intervals (15s) — the UC3 leak curve needs to surface within
# seconds of the participant starting the loop, not on the operator-style 60s
# default.
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: prometheus-server
  namespace: artemis-observability
  labels:
    app: prometheus-server
    kagent-workshop/component: observability
data:
  prometheus.yml: |
    global:
      scrape_interval: 15s
      evaluation_interval: 15s

    scrape_configs:
      - job_name: prometheus
        static_configs:
          - targets: ['localhost:9090']

      - job_name: kubernetes-services
        kubernetes_sd_configs:
          - role: endpoints
        relabel_configs:
          # Keep only endpoints whose backing Service carries monitoring=prom.
          - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_service_label_monitoring]
            action: keep
            regex: prom
          # If the Service annotates a custom metrics path, honour it.
          - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_service_annotation_prometheus_io_path]
            action: replace
            target_label: __metrics_path__
            regex: (.+)
          # Surface namespace + service name as labels on every series so the
          # UC3 agent can query `process_resident_memory_bytes{kubernetes_service_name="..."}`.
          - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_namespace]
            action: replace
            target_label: kubernetes_namespace
          - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_service_name]
            action: replace
            target_label: kubernetes_service_name
          - source_labels: [__meta_kubernetes_endpoint_port_name]
            action: replace
            target_label: kubernetes_port_name

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: prometheus-server
  namespace: artemis-observability
  labels:
    app: prometheus-server
    kagent-workshop/component: observability
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: prometheus-server
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: prometheus-server
        kagent-workshop/component: observability
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: prometheus-server
      containers:
        - name: prometheus
          image: prom/prometheus:v2.55.1
          args:
            - --config.file=/etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml
            - --storage.tsdb.path=/prometheus
            - --storage.tsdb.retention.time=2h
            - --web.enable-lifecycle
          ports:
            - name: http
              containerPort: 9090
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /-/ready
              port: http
            periodSeconds: 5
            initialDelaySeconds: 5
          livenessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /-/healthy
              port: http
            periodSeconds: 30
            initialDelaySeconds: 30
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: "100m"
              memory: "128Mi"
            limits:
              cpu: "500m"
              memory: "256Mi"
          volumeMounts:
            - name: config
              mountPath: /etc/prometheus
            - name: tsdb
              mountPath: /prometheus
      volumes:
        - name: config
          configMap:
            name: prometheus-server
        - name: tsdb
          emptyDir: {}

apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: prometheus-server
  namespace: artemis-observability
  labels:
    app: prometheus-server
    kagent-workshop/component: observability
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  selector:
    app: prometheus-server
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 9090
      targetPort: http
      protocol: TCP

# Grafana provisioned datasource.
#
# Mounted at /etc/grafana/provisioning/datasources/ — Grafana picks it up at
# boot via its filesystem provisioning mechanism (apiVersion: 1). The kagent
# pre-packaged Grafana sub-agent (FR-012 AC, wired by STORY-019) doesn't have
# to name the datasource by ID because isDefault: true is set.
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: grafana-datasources
  namespace: artemis-observability
  labels:
    app: grafana
    kagent-workshop/component: observability
data:
  datasources.yaml: |
    apiVersion: 1
    datasources:
      - name: Prometheus
        type: prometheus
        access: proxy
        url: http://prometheus-server.artemis-observability.svc.cluster.local:9090
        isDefault: true
        editable: false
        jsonData:
          timeInterval: 15s

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: grafana
  namespace: artemis-observability
  labels:
    app: grafana
    kagent-workshop/component: observability
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: grafana
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: grafana
        kagent-workshop/component: observability
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: grafana
          image: grafana/grafana-oss:11.3.0
          # Anonymous-admin: workshop runs in per-participant vClusters; the
          # Service is ClusterIP-only and reached via kubectl port-forward, so
          # no cross-tenant exposure path exists. The "this is not how you'd
          # run Grafana in production" disclaimer lives in uc3/README.md
          # (STORY-021).
          env:
            - name: GF_AUTH_ANONYMOUS_ENABLED
              value: "true"
            - name: GF_AUTH_ANONYMOUS_ORG_ROLE
              value: Admin
            - name: GF_AUTH_DISABLE_LOGIN_FORM
              value: "true"
            - name: GF_AUTH_BASIC_ENABLED
              value: "false"
          ports:
            - name: http
              containerPort: 3000
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /api/health
              port: http
            periodSeconds: 5
            initialDelaySeconds: 5
          livenessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /api/health
              port: http
            periodSeconds: 30
            initialDelaySeconds: 30
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: "50m"
              memory: "64Mi"
            limits:
              cpu: "500m"
              memory: "256Mi"
          volumeMounts:
            - name: datasources
              mountPath: /etc/grafana/provisioning/datasources
            - name: storage
              mountPath: /var/lib/grafana
      volumes:
        - name: datasources
          configMap:
            name: grafana-datasources
        - name: storage
          emptyDir: {}

apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: grafana
  namespace: artemis-observability
  labels:
    app: grafana
    kagent-workshop/component: observability
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  selector:
    app: grafana
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 3000
      targetPort: http
      protocol: TCP

# kagent ↔ artemis-observability namespace bridge.
#
# kagent v0.9.0's pre-packaged tools + the kagent-grafana-mcp RemoteMCPServer
# resolve Prom/Graf at {prometheus,grafana}.kagent.svc.cluster.local — bound at
# helm install time. STORY-018 deliberately put the actual pods in
# artemis-observability (per docs/artemis-naming.md). These two ExternalName
# Services reconcile the gap with a DNS CNAME indirection that is invisible to
# kagent's runtime, requires no helm upgrade, and survives kagent reinstalls.
#
# Originally shipped under uc3/agents/ by STORY-019. Promoted to
# infra/observability/ by STORY-025 once UC4 confirmed identical bridge needs
# (UC4's coordinator delegates to UC3's rover-telemetry-debugger which, via
# a2a, delegates to kagent's observability-agent — same DNS dependency).
#
# Why ExternalName and not a Helm-values override:
# - Override path requires `helm upgrade` on every kagent install (also fixes
#   the STORY-018-flagged `make kagent-install` non-idempotency, but in a
#   different scope). Bridge path is surgical and scoped to the observability
#   bundle's lifecycle.
# - Bridge Services delete cleanly with `make observability-down`, keeping
#   kagent's own state untouched.
#
# Not inside infra/observability/kustomization.yaml because the kustomization
# sets `namespace: artemis-observability` at the root level — that would
# override these Services' `namespace: kagent` and silently break the bridge.
# Applied as a separate `kubectl apply -f` by the Makefile `observability-up`
# target instead.
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: prometheus
  namespace: kagent
  labels:
    kagent-workshop/component: observability
    kagent-workshop/role: bridge
spec:
  type: ExternalName
  externalName: prometheus-server.artemis-observability.svc.cluster.local
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 9090
      targetPort: 9090
      protocol: TCP
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: grafana
  namespace: kagent
  labels:
    kagent-workshop/component: observability
    kagent-workshop/role: bridge
spec:
  type: ExternalName
  externalName: grafana.artemis-observability.svc.cluster.local
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 3000
      targetPort: 3000
      protocol: TCP

# Configures kagent-grafana-mcp's Secret + ConfigMap (which kagent's helm install
# creates empty) with a real Grafana service-account token and the in-cluster
# Grafana URL, then rollout-restarts the kagent-grafana-mcp Deployment so its
# pod picks up the new envFrom values.
#
# Anonymous-admin Grafana (see infra/observability/grafana/30-deployment.yaml —
# GF_AUTH_ANONYMOUS_ENABLED + GF_AUTH_ANONYMOUS_ORG_ROLE=Admin) lets us create
# the service account + token without baking a Grafana admin password into a
# Secret. The per-vCluster network is the trust boundary.
#
# Applied as a standalone YAML (NOT via the kustomization) because the
# kustomization's root namespace override (artemis-observability) would mangle
# every resource here — they all live in `kagent` ns. Same pattern as
# kagent-bridge-services.yaml.
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: kagent-grafana-mcp-setup
  namespace: kagent
  labels:
    kagent-workshop/component: observability
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  name: kagent-grafana-mcp-setup
  namespace: kagent
  labels:
    kagent-workshop/component: observability
rules:
  # Patch the existing kagent-installed Secret + ConfigMap in place.
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources: ["secrets", "configmaps"]
    verbs: ["get", "patch"]
  # Trigger a rollout restart on the kagent-grafana-mcp Deployment so it
  # re-reads its envFrom after the patch.
  - apiGroups: ["apps"]
    resources: ["deployments"]
    verbs: ["get", "patch"]
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: kagent-grafana-mcp-setup
  namespace: kagent
  labels:
    kagent-workshop/component: observability
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: Role
  name: kagent-grafana-mcp-setup
subjects:
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: kagent-grafana-mcp-setup
    namespace: kagent
---
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: kagent-grafana-mcp-setup
  namespace: kagent
  labels:
    kagent-workshop/component: observability
spec:
  backoffLimit: 4
  activeDeadlineSeconds: 180
  ttlSecondsAfterFinished: 600
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        kagent-workshop/component: observability
        app: kagent-grafana-mcp-setup
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: kagent-grafana-mcp-setup
      restartPolicy: OnFailure
      containers:
        - name: setup
          image: rg.fr-par.scw.cloud/apogasa/kubectl:latest
          command: ["/bin/sh", "-eu", "-o", "pipefail", "-c"]
          args:
            - |
              # apogasa/kubectl:latest is busybox-based — it has wget + kubectl
              # but not curl. wget syntax below uses busybox-supported flags.

              GRAFANA_URL="http://grafana.kagent.svc.cluster.local:3000"
              SA_NAME="kagent-mcp"
              TOKEN_NAME="mcp-token-$(date +%s)"

              echo "[grafana-mcp-setup] waiting for Grafana to be ready at ${GRAFANA_URL}..."
              for i in $(seq 1 60); do
                if wget -q -O /dev/null "${GRAFANA_URL}/api/health" 2>/dev/null; then
                  echo "[grafana-mcp-setup] grafana ready after ${i}s"
                  break
                fi
                sleep 2
              done

              # Anonymous admin permits SA + token creation without explicit
              # credentials. Find existing SA first (idempotent re-run); create
              # if not found.
              echo "[grafana-mcp-setup] looking up service account '${SA_NAME}'..."
              SA_LIST=$(wget -q -O - \
                "${GRAFANA_URL}/api/serviceaccounts/search?query=${SA_NAME}" \
                2>/dev/null || echo '{"serviceAccounts":[]}')
              SA_ID=$(echo "${SA_LIST}" | sed -nE 's/.*"id":([0-9]+)[^}]*"name":"'"${SA_NAME}"'".*/\1/p' | head -1)

              if [ -z "${SA_ID}" ]; then
                echo "[grafana-mcp-setup] creating service account '${SA_NAME}'..."
                SA_RESP=$(wget -q -O - \
                  --post-data="{\"name\":\"${SA_NAME}\",\"role\":\"Admin\",\"isDisabled\":false}" \
                  --header='Content-Type: application/json' \
                  "${GRAFANA_URL}/api/serviceaccounts" 2>&1)
                SA_ID=$(echo "${SA_RESP}" | sed -nE 's/.*"id":([0-9]+).*/\1/p' | head -1)
                if [ -z "${SA_ID}" ]; then
                  echo "[grafana-mcp-setup] FATAL: cannot create SA. Response: ${SA_RESP}"
                  exit 1
                fi
                echo "[grafana-mcp-setup] SA created with id=${SA_ID}"
              else
                echo "[grafana-mcp-setup] reusing existing SA id=${SA_ID}"
              fi

              echo "[grafana-mcp-setup] creating new token..."
              TOKEN_RESP=$(wget -q -O - \
                --post-data="{\"name\":\"${TOKEN_NAME}\"}" \
                --header='Content-Type: application/json' \
                "${GRAFANA_URL}/api/serviceaccounts/${SA_ID}/tokens" 2>&1)
              TOKEN=$(echo "${TOKEN_RESP}" | sed -nE 's/.*"key":"([^"]+)".*/\1/p' | head -1)
              if [ -z "${TOKEN}" ]; then
                echo "[grafana-mcp-setup] FATAL: cannot create token. Response: ${TOKEN_RESP}"
                exit 1
              fi
              echo "[grafana-mcp-setup] token created (${#TOKEN} bytes)"

              # base64 the token for stringData → data conversion via strategic
              # merge patch on Secret.
              TOKEN_B64=$(printf '%s' "${TOKEN}" | base64 -w0 2>/dev/null || printf '%s' "${TOKEN}" | base64)

              echo "[grafana-mcp-setup] patching Secret kagent/kagent-grafana-mcp..."
              kubectl patch secret kagent-grafana-mcp -n kagent \
                --type=merge \
                -p "{\"data\":{\"GRAFANA_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN\":\"${TOKEN_B64}\"}}"

              echo "[grafana-mcp-setup] patching ConfigMap kagent/kagent-grafana-mcp..."
              kubectl patch configmap kagent-grafana-mcp -n kagent \
                --type=merge \
                -p "{\"data\":{\"GRAFANA_URL\":\"${GRAFANA_URL}\"}}"

              echo "[grafana-mcp-setup] rollout-restart kagent-grafana-mcp..."
              kubectl rollout restart deployment kagent-grafana-mcp -n kagent

              echo "[grafana-mcp-setup] done"
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: "10m"
              memory: "32Mi"
            limits:
              cpu: "200m"
              memory: "128Mi"

apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: artemis-uc3
  labels:
    kagent-workshop/uc: uc3
    kagent-workshop/scenario: oomkilled

apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: lunar-rover-telemetry
  namespace: artemis-uc3
  labels:
    app: lunar-rover-telemetry
    kagent-workshop/uc: uc3
    # Picked up by infra/observability/'s Prometheus kubernetes_sd via the
    # `keep` relabel rule on `monitoring=prom` (STORY-018).
    monitoring: prom
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  selector:
    app: lunar-rover-telemetry
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 8000
      targetPort: http
      protocol: TCP

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: lunar-rover-telemetry
  namespace: artemis-uc3
  labels:
    app: lunar-rover-telemetry
    kagent-workshop/uc: uc3
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: lunar-rover-telemetry
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: lunar-rover-telemetry
    spec:
      # Deliberately no readinessProbe / livenessProbe — under memory pressure
      # a slow probe would race with the OOM cycle and surface as a probe
      # failure event instead of OOMKilled, masking UC3's friction signal.
      # NFR-003: the broken state must be manifest-side, no probe-flap.
      containers:
        - name: lunar-rover-telemetry
          # Real published tag — semver-style for this app variant
          # (UC1's mission-control uses :v1 / :v999; lunar-rover-telemetry
          # publishes :v1.0.0). The leak symptom is driven by the 64Mi
          # limit below + participant-triggered POST /leak
          # (apps/lunar-rover-telemetry/main.py), not by anything image-side.
          image: rg.fr-par.scw.cloud/apogasa/lunar-rover-telemetry:v1.0.0
          ports:
            - name: http
              containerPort: 8000
          env:
            - name: APP_IDENTITY
              value: lunar-rover-telemetry
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: "10m"
              memory: "32Mi"
            limits:
              cpu: "500m"
              # The OOM driver: 64Mi is below the working set ~70 calls to
              # POST /leak produces (1 MiB per call), so the kernel reaps
              # the container deterministically (FR-012 AC).
              memory: "64Mi"

# UC3 diagnostic agent — diagnoses OOMKilled on the lunar-rover-telemetry
# Deployment, composing kagent's pre-packaged promql-agent + observability-agent
# (a2a delegation) with K8s read tools from kagent-tool-server.
#
# Tool surface (per FR-008-style "smallest set sufficient", scaled for FR-012):
#   - kubectl read tools:        k8s_get_resources, k8s_describe_resource, k8s_get_events
#                                (sourced from kagent-tool-server RemoteMCPServer)
#   - PromQL generation (a2a):   promql-agent (kagent v0.9.0 demo profile)
#   - Grafana + Prom execution
#     + dashboard creation (a2a): observability-agent (kagent v0.9.0 demo profile)
#
# The a2a delegation pattern (`tools[].type: Agent` + `tools[].agent.name: <agent>`)
# was confirmed against kagent v0.9.0's own observability-agent.spec — see
# STORY-019 §Spike findings.
#
# No custom Prometheus or Grafana agent is shipped (FR-012 AC, "no reinvention"
# constraint from the brief). UC3 reuses upstream kagent agents verbatim.
#
# kagent's pre-packaged agents and tools resolve Prom/Graf at
# {prometheus,grafana}.kagent.svc.cluster.local — STORY-018 puts the actual
# pods in artemis-observability. The bridge Services in
# infra/observability/kagent-bridge-services.yaml (promoted from uc3/agents/
# by STORY-025) reconcile the namespace gap via ExternalName CNAME indirection.
apiVersion: kagent.dev/v1alpha2
kind: Agent
metadata:
  name: artemis-rover-telemetry-debugger
  namespace: kagent
  labels:
    kagent-workshop/uc: uc3
spec:
  type: Declarative
  description: |
    Diagnoses why the Artemis `lunar-rover-telemetry` Deployment in the
    `artemis-uc3` namespace is not keeping its uplink up. Correlates
    Kubernetes Pod state with a Grafana memory time-series and synthesises
    the root cause in one step.
  declarative:
    modelConfig: default-model-config
    systemMessage: |
      You are an Artemis lunar rover telemetry on-call SRE. Your job is to
      diagnose why the `lunar-rover-telemetry` Deployment in the
      `artemis-uc3` namespace is restarting, and report a single sentence
      of root cause plus a one-line remediation hint.

      Diagnostic loop:
        1. `k8s_get_resources` with kind=Pod and namespace=artemis-uc3 — list
           the pods in the namespace. Pick the `lunar-rover-telemetry-…` pod.
        2. `k8s_describe_resource` with kind=Pod, namespace=artemis-uc3, and
           the pod name from step 1. Look at
           `lastState.terminated.reason` — if it says `OOMKilled`, you have
           the symptom. Note the container's `resources.limits.memory`.
        3. Delegate to the `observability-agent` to build a Grafana
           dashboard panel showing
           `process_resident_memory_bytes{kubernetes_namespace="artemis-uc3",
           kubernetes_service_name="lunar-rover-telemetry"}` over the last
           10 minutes. Capture the dashboard URL the sub-agent returns.
        4. Surface that URL to the user — the participant clicks through to
           see the monotone leak curve.
        5. Finish with one root-cause sentence (e.g. "the container's
           memory limit (64 Mi) is below the working set the leak loop
           produces, so the kernel OOM-kills it before the loop completes")
           and one remediation hint ("raise `resources.limits.memory` or
           fix the leak in the application code").

      Tone: concise. The participant is running through a 5-minute tour
      and needs the answer, not a tutorial. Do NOT explain Kubernetes
      basics or PromQL syntax unless the user explicitly asks.

      Tool budget: at most 1 `k8s_get_resources` + 1 `k8s_describe_resource`
      call, 1 delegation to observability-agent, 0 other tool calls. If a
      single iteration is insufficient, prefer asking the user a clarifying
      question over running more tools.
    tools:
      - type: McpServer
        mcpServer:
          apiGroup: kagent.dev
          kind: RemoteMCPServer
          name: kagent-tool-server
          namespace: kagent
          toolNames:
            - k8s_get_resources
            - k8s_describe_resource
            - k8s_get_events
      - type: Agent
        agent:
          name: promql-agent
      - type: Agent
        agent:
          name: observability-agent

commands.label: Configure the kagent Grafana MCP (service account + token)
Wait for the Grafana MCP setup to converge
Wait for the rover to come online
Stream 30 telemetry bursts to the rover
commands.command: kubectl run telemetry-stream --rm -i --restart=Never --image=curlimages/curl:8.10.1 --namespace=artemis-uc3 --command -- sh -c 'for i in $(seq 1 30); do curl -sf -X POST http://lunar-rover-telemetry:8000/leak --max-time 2; echo; done; echo stream complete'

steps.title: Mission status check — is the rover keeping its uplink up?
steps.explanation:
The telemetry stream just cut out partway through. Check the rover's status to see whether the uplink came back up on its own.

Look at the **`RESTARTS`** column carefully — a healthy rover stays at zero across a single mission window. If the count ticked up, the rover is alive but it didn't survive the stream, and that's the cue to call the agent in.

steps.title: Call the agent for help
steps.explanation:
The rover restarted on us mid-stream and we don't yet know why. Hand the diagnosis to **`artemis-rover-telemetry-debugger`** through the kagent dashboard.

Use the **dashboard** button in this workshop-tour extension to open the kagent web dashboard in your browser. In the dashboard, open the chat for **`artemis-rover-telemetry-debugger`** (namespace `kagent`) and paste the following prompt:

> The lunar-rover-telemetry pod in the artemis-uc3 namespace keeps restarting. Diagnose it, and show me a memory chart from the time of the failure.

The agent has the same Kubernetes read tools you would have used by hand (`describe pod`, `get events`) **plus** sub-agents that talk to Prometheus and Grafana directly. Read the response in the chat surface — it will name the root cause in one sentence and surface a Grafana dashboard URL you can click through to see the memory curve from the failure window.

steps.title: What we'd have done by hand
steps.explanation:
Without the agent, you would have run three `kubectl` commands to localise the failure inside the cluster and then a separate set of Grafana clicks to correlate it with telemetry. Five surfaces, one synthesis to do yourself.

The `kubectl` half:

```bash
kubectl get pods -n artemis-uc3 -l app=lunar-rover-telemetry
kubectl describe pod -n artemis-uc3 -l app=lunar-rover-telemetry
kubectl get events -n artemis-uc3 --sort-by=.lastTimestamp
```

Three pieces of state to combine into a verdict: a `Running 1/1` Pod whose previous container terminated with `lastState.terminated.reason: OOMKilled` and `exitCode: 137`, kubelet events naming the OOM, and the Pod's restart count ticking up by one. The cluster says *the rover ran out of room and the kernel reaped it*, but you have to read three commands to hear it.

The Grafana half — what the agent did on your behalf:

- Connected to the cluster's Prometheus through the kagent observability sub-agent.
- Generated and executed the PromQL query `process_resident_memory_bytes{kubernetes_namespace="artemis-uc3", kubernetes_service_name="lunar-rover-telemetry"}` over the failure window.
- Built a Grafana dashboard panel showing the memory curve climbing from idle to the 64 Mi container limit, then dropping to zero on each OOM cycle.
- Returned the dashboard URL in the chat surface for you to click.

The agent ran the same three kubectl tools and the same observability tooling and returned a single sentence of root cause: **the rover's container memory limit (64 Mi) is below the working set the telemetry stream produces, so the kernel reaps the container before the stream completes**. You ran one `kubectl get pods` just to see the restart count was off — you didn't have to walk the rest, and you didn't have to switch context to Grafana.

Open the dashboard URL the agent returned in the chat above to see the memory curve for yourself; it is the same evidence a human on-call would have pieced together by hand, only the agent did the piecing.

Multiplied across the dozen incidents an on-call rotation handles in a week, that's the value the workshop is selling.
